body:
## Summary
- Add a Project planning model doc with the agreed Status, Priority, Effort, and optional Release fields.
- Clarify that `agent`, `agent/*`, and `agent-running/*` remain operational labels, while `priority/*` and `effort/*` are legacy/fallback opt-in labels.
- Update the project-manager prompt, workflow text, label descriptions, and focused coverage to reflect the boundary without adding Project creation or sync.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/apply-project-management-labels-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/project-management-labels.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #140

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates docs alongside workflow/prompt/CLI wording: [project-planning.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/351e6d6052eb2589b6da4507317eb3593c4ca6d0/.agent/docs/architecture/project-planning.md), [supported-workflows.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/351e6d6052eb2589b6da4507317eb3593c4ca6d0/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md), and [configuration-list.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/351e6d6052eb2589b6da4507317eb3593c4ca6d0/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes [#140](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/140), which is scoped under parent [#126](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/126). The implementation matches the requested minimal fields and legacy/fallback label boundary from that context. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to project-planning docs, project-manager prompt/workflow text, label descriptions, CLI wording, and focused tests. No unrelated route or sync implementation was added. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Keeps the existing project-manager label-plan parser/apply path and only renames its surface as legacy/fallback in [apply-project-management-labels.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/351e6d6052eb2589b6da4507317eb3593c4ca6d0/.agent/src/cli/apply-project-management-labels.ts) and [project-management-labels.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/351e6d6052eb2589b6da4507317eb3593c4ca6d0/.agent/src/project-management-labels.ts). |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implements the agreed boundary through docs, prompt text, constants, and tests without adding Project creation, Project sync, repo variable mutation, or a new route. |
| communication/generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 6/6 | User-facing project-manager output remains compact; status wording changes from generic managed labels to “legacy managed label application” without adding verbose log-like output. |
| coding_workflow/generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | New docs use a planning-field table and structural explanation, not a per-file inventory table. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are that PR #141 stays tightly aligned with child issue #140: define the minimal Project model, preserve operational labels, and mark `priority/*` / `effort/*` as legacy/fallback.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #141 closes #140, a child task under #126 for simplifying Sepo’s project-planning model.
- It documents the minimal GitHub Project fields: Status, Priority, Effort, and optional Release.
- It clarifies that `agent`, `agent/*`, and `agent-running/*` remain operational labels, while `priority/*` and `effort/*` are legacy/fallback opt-in labels.
- It updates docs, prompt/workflow wording, label descriptions, CLI output text, and focused tests without adding Project creation or sync behavior.

## Review
All reviewer outputs agree that the PR is scoped correctly and does not have actionable defects. The only extra notes were cosmetic or explicitly deferred by the current issue scope.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues found | INFO | The PR matches #140/#126, keeps Project creation/sync out of scope, and has passing focused coverage. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific findings.
- Code review found no blocking or warning-level issues.
- Existing inline PR comments were empty, so no duplicate feedback needed to be avoided.
- Project field sync, Project creation, and migration of existing label descriptions remain intentionally out of scope.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved actionable branch changes remain, so automation should not run another fix pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

title: Define minimal GitHub Project planning model
